description: Recreate the Next.js Morse Code Studio app 1:1 in the Expo mobile app with decoder, encoder, spectrogram, sessions, and auth

userStories.title: Add new dependencies to Expo app
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all required native dependencies installed so that audio, canvas, and file system features work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to apps/expo/package.json dependencies: react-native-live-audio-stream, react-native-audio-api, @shopify/react-native-skia, expo-document-picker, expo-clipboard, expo-file-system, expo-sharing, @react-native-community/slider
Move @morse-bot/morse-decoder from devDependencies to dependencies in apps/expo/package.json
Dependencies are sorted alphabetically in package.json (sherif enforces this)
pnpm install succeeds without errors
Typecheck passes: pnpm --filter @morse-bot/expo typecheck
userStories.notes: The monorepo uses pnpm with catalog: versions in pnpm-workspace.yaml. For packages not in the catalog, use explicit versions. sherif enforces alphabetical ordering in package.json deps.

userStories.title: Update Expo app.config.ts with microphone permissions and plugins
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Expo config updated with required native module plugins and permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/expo/app.config.ts: add iOS NSMicrophoneUsageDescription permission string
apps/expo/app.config.ts: add Android RECORD_AUDIO permission
Add required Expo plugins for new native modules (react-native-live-audio-stream, @shopify/react-native-skia, etc.)
Update app name to 'Morse Code Studio'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check existing app.config.ts for the current plugin/permission format and follow the same pattern.

userStories.title: Create tab navigation structure and remove posts demo
userStories.description: As a user, I want tab navigation between Decoder and Encoder modes, replacing the demo posts UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx with expo-router Tabs component, two tabs: 'Decoder' (index) and 'Encoder'
Tab bar at bottom of screen with descriptive labels
Decoder tab is the default/initial screen
Delete apps/expo/src/app/index.tsx (old posts home screen)
Delete apps/expo/src/app/post/[id].tsx (old post detail screen)
Create placeholder apps/expo/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx that renders a SafeAreaView with Text 'Decoder' as temporary content
Create placeholder apps/expo/src/app/(tabs)/encoder.tsx that renders a SafeAreaView with Text 'Encoder' as temporary content
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Follow expo-router file-based routing conventions. The (tabs) directory creates a tab navigator. Root _layout.tsx already has QueryClientProvider - keep that wrapper.

userStories.title: Add auth-aware header with Sessions navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the app title and navigate to My Sessions when signed in.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify apps/expo/src/app/_layout.tsx: update Stack screenOptions to show 'Morse Code Studio' as header title
Add 'My Sessions' header button/link visible only when authenticated (use authClient.useSession() from ~/utils/auth)
My Sessions link navigates to /sessions route
Add Sign in / Sign out button in header using existing Discord OAuth flow (authClient.signIn.social / authClient.signOut)
Create apps/expo/src/app/sessions.tsx as placeholder screen with Text 'Sessions' and back navigation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auth is already configured in apps/expo/src/utils/auth.ts with Better Auth + @better-auth/expo plugin. Use authClient.useSession() for session state.

userStories.title: Create WAV file parser utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure-JS WAV parser that works in React Native to decode WAV audio files to raw PCM samples.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/utils/wav-parser.ts accepting ArrayBuffer input
Returns { samples: Float32Array, sampleRate: number, channels: number, bitsPerSample: number }
Supports 16-bit and 32-bit PCM WAV files (format code 1)
Extracts first channel only (mono output)
Normalizes samples to [-1, 1] range (Int16 / 32768 or Int32 / 2147483648)
Throws descriptive errors for: missing RIFF header, missing WAVE format, unsupported audio format, missing fmt chunk, missing data chunk, unsupported bit depth
Logic adapted from packages/morse-decoder/src/__tests__/helpers/read-wav.ts but without node:fs dependency
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing read-wav.ts in the test helpers uses DataView-based RIFF/WAV parsing. Port the parsing logic but accept ArrayBuffer instead of file path. Do NOT use .js extensions in imports.

userStories.title: Create useMorseDecoder hook for React Native
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the useMorseDecoder hook ported to React Native so audio samples can be decoded into morse text.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/hooks/use-morse-decoder.ts
Uses @morse-bot/morse-decoder createDecoder, DEFAULT_CONFIG, and type imports (DecoderConfig, DecoderStats, MorseElement)
Hook returns { decodedText, stats, currentElements, processSamples, reset, updateConfig } matching web API
Uses requestAnimationFrame batching for state updates (same pattern as apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-morse-decoder.ts)
Decoder recreates when config changes using JSON.stringify configKey comparison
Cancels pending rAF on unmount
No 'use client' directive (not needed in React Native)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a near-direct port from apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-morse-decoder.ts. requestAnimationFrame and cancelAnimationFrame are available in React Native. Remove the 'use client' directive. All @morse-bot/morse-decoder imports work unchanged (pure JS package).

userStories.title: Create useAudioInput hook for microphone recording
userStories.description: As a user, I want to record audio from my phone's microphone to decode morse code in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/hooks/use-audio-input.ts using react-native-live-audio-stream
Configure stream for 8000 Hz sample rate, 1 channel (mono), 16-bit PCM
Convert incoming base64 PCM data to Float32Array: decode base64 -> Uint8Array -> DataView -> read Int16 pairs -> divide by 32768
Call onSamples(float32Array) callback with each chunk of converted samples
Request microphone permission before recording starts (use PermissionsAndroid on Android or expo-av Audio.requestPermissionsAsync)
Returns { isRecording, error, startRecording, stopRecording } matching web hook interface
Shows 'Microphone permission denied' error message if permission is not granted
Cleans up stream on stopRecording and on component unmount
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: react-native-live-audio-stream emits 'data' events with base64-encoded PCM. The conversion chain: base64 string -> byte array -> Int16Array (little-endian) -> Float32Array (normalize by dividing by 32768). Use a ref for the onSamples callback to avoid stale closures.

userStories.title: Create useAudioFile hook for WAV file decoding
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pick a WAV file from my device to decode pre-recorded morse code audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/hooks/use-audio-file.ts using expo-document-picker and expo-file-system
processFile() opens native file picker via DocumentPicker.getDocumentAsync({ type: ['audio/wav', 'audio/x-wav'] })
Reads selected file as base64 via FileSystem.readAsStringAsync(uri, { encoding: FileSystem.EncodingType.Base64 })
Converts base64 to ArrayBuffer, parses with wav-parser.ts from ~/utils/wav-parser
Delivers samples in chunks of 4096 to onSamples(chunk, timestampMs) callback
Reports progress (0-1) between chunks
Yields to UI thread between chunks via setTimeout(0)
Returns { isProcessing, progress, error, processFile, stopProcessing }
Cancellation via stopProcessing() stops mid-processing using a cancelledRef
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-audio-file.ts for chunking and progress logic. The main difference is file reading: instead of File.arrayBuffer(), use expo-document-picker + expo-file-system. Base64 to ArrayBuffer conversion: atob() or Buffer.from(base64, 'base64') in RN.

userStories.title: Create decoder controls component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to adjust decoder parameters (frequency, speed, threshold, adaptive mode) to tune decoding.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/components/decoder-controls.tsx with React Native controls
Frequency: @react-native-community/slider, range 300-1200 Hz, step 10, shows current value as Text
Speed (WPM): custom picker or Pressable dropdown with presets [5, 10, 13, 15, 20, 25, 30]
Threshold: @react-native-community/slider, range 0.001-0.1, step 0.001, shows value formatted to 3 decimal places
Adaptive toggle: React Native Switch component
Clear/Reset button as Pressable with styling
All controls accept isDisabled prop and are disabled during active decoding
Calls onUpdateConfig(partial: Partial<DecoderConfig>) on each change
Calls onReset() when Clear button pressed
Uses NativeWind classes for styling consistent with existing Expo app patterns
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port logic from apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/decoder-controls.tsx. Replace HTML elements: <input type='range'> -> Slider, <select> -> custom picker using Modal+FlatList or simple Pressable dropdown, <input type='checkbox'> -> Switch. Import DEFAULT_CONFIG from @morse-bot/morse-decoder for initial values.

userStories.title: Create signal statistics display component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see real-time signal statistics during decoding.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/components/signal-stats.tsx with React Native Views and Text
Displays 4 stats in a 2x2 grid: Signal (dBFS), Frequency (Hz), SNR (dB), Speed (WPM)
Shows '-- dBFS' / '-- dB' when values are not finite (use isFinite() check)
Color-coded values using NativeWind theme tokens: text-primary, text-secondary, text-chart-5, text-accent-foreground
Each stat has uppercase label in small text and value in monospace font
Accepts stats: DecoderStats prop from @morse-bot/morse-decoder
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Direct port from apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/signal-stats.tsx. Replace <div>/<span> with <View>/<Text>. Same StatItem pattern. NativeWind supports the same Tailwind class names.

userStories.title: Create spectrogram visualization with Skia
userStories.description: As a user, I want a scrolling waterfall spectrogram to visually monitor the morse signal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/components/spectrogram.tsx using @shopify/react-native-skia Canvas
Maintains a circular buffer of 512 dB values
On each stats update, appends new signalDb value and shifts buffer left
Draws 512 vertical rectangles colored by dbToColor() function
dbToColor() gradient: black -> green -> yellow for dBFS range -80 to -20 (port directly from apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/spectrogram.tsx)
Canvas renders via Skia (separate thread, does not block JS)
Accepts stats: DecoderStats prop
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Skia Canvas with useCanvasRef or imperative drawing. The dbToColor() function is pure math and ports directly. For drawing, use canvas.drawRect() for each column with Skia.Paint colored by the dB value.

userStories.title: Create decoded text output component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see decoded morse text with a blinking cursor and copy support.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/components/decoded-text.tsx with ScrollView
Shows decoded text in monospace font (font-mono NativeWind class)
Shows in-progress morse elements (dit/dah) as dots/dashes in muted color using elementsToSymbols() helper
Blinking cursor (animated opacity View) shown when isRecording is true
Auto-scrolls to bottom when decodedText or currentElements change (scrollViewRef.current?.scrollToEnd())
Copy button that copies decodedText to clipboard via expo-clipboard setStringAsync()
Shows 'Listening for morse code...' placeholder when recording with empty output
Shows 'Press Start to begin decoding' when idle with empty output
Wrapped in a card-like View with rounded corners and background
Accepts props: decodedText: string, currentElements: MorseElement[], isRecording: boolean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/decoded-text.tsx. Replace navigator.clipboard with expo-clipboard. Replace div ref auto-scroll with ScrollView ref scrollToEnd(). elementsToSymbols maps 'dit'->'.' and 'dah'->'-'.

userStories.title: Create decoder panel - main decoder screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want the complete decoder interface combining controls, audio input, visualization, and output.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/components/decoder-panel.tsx orchestrating all decoder components
Uses useMorseDecoder hook for decoding state
Uses useAudioInput hook for microphone recording
Uses useAudioFile hook for file processing
'Start Mic' / 'Stop Mic' toggle button (destructive variant when recording)
'Open File' button that triggers file picker
'Stop' button visible during file processing
Progress bar (View with dynamic width and bg-primary) shown during file processing
Error messages shown for mic/file errors
Renders DecoderControls, Spectrogram, SignalStats, and DecodedText components
Updates decoder sampleRate when mic provides actualSampleRate (if applicable)
Mic and file buttons disabled appropriately during active decoding
Uses authClient.useSession() to check authentication state
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/decoder-panel.tsx. This is the main orchestrator. Replace HTML <Button> with Pressable, <input type='file'> with useAudioFile().processFile(). The save session button will be added in a later story.

userStories.title: Create useMorsePlayer hook for encoder
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the useMorsePlayer hook ported to React Native for morse code audio playback.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/hooks/use-morse-player.ts using react-native-audio-api
Import AudioContext from react-native-audio-api (Web Audio API compatible)
play(text) creates AudioContext, schedules OscillatorNode tones at specified frequency and WPM
Oscillators use sine wave type with 0.3 gain
Tone scheduling uses AudioContext.currentTime for precise timing with 50ms lead time
stop() clears all scheduled timeouts, closes AudioContext, resets state
currentCharIndex updates via setTimeout during playback for UI highlighting
buildCharTimings() function ported directly from apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-morse-player.ts (pure math using ditDurationMs, TIMING_RATIOS, getMorseForChar from @morse-bot/morse-decoder)
Returns { isPlaying, currentCharIndex, play, stop }
Cleanup timeout on playback completion (totalDurationMs + 100ms buffer)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: react-native-audio-api provides AudioContext, OscillatorNode, GainNode matching Web Audio API. The hook logic from apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-morse-player.ts should port nearly 1:1. buildCharTimings is 100% portable (pure math).

userStories.title: Create encoder panel component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type text and hear it played back as morse code audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/expo/src/components/encoder-panel.tsx with React Native components
Multiline TextInput for text entry with placeholder 'Type text to encode as morse code...'
WPM speed picker with same presets as decoder: [5, 10, 13, 15, 20, 25, 30]
Frequency display in Hz (accepts frequency prop)
Play button (disabled when text is empty or trimmed empty)
Stop button with destructive styling (shown during playback, replaces Play)
Real-time character highlighting: map over text chars, current char gets bg-primary text-primary-foreground styling
TextInput disabled during playback
Character highlight display shown only during playback when chars exist
Uses useMorsePlayer hook for audio playback
Accepts props: frequency: number, wpm: number
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/encoder-panel.tsx. Replace <textarea> with <TextInput multiline>. Replace <select> with same picker pattern used in decoder-controls. Character highlighting uses map over [...text] to create individually styled Text components.

userStories.title: Wire up tab screens with decoder and encoder panels
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Decoder and Encoder tabs to show the full functional panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/expo/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx: render DecoderPanel inside SafeAreaView, set screen title 'Morse Decoder'
Update apps/expo/src/app/(tabs)/encoder.tsx: render EncoderPanel inside SafeAreaView, set screen title 'Morse Encoder'
Encoder receives frequency and wpm as props (use state with defaults from DEFAULT_CONFIG)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replace the placeholder content from US-003 with the actual panel components. Use Stack.Screen options for title. Import DEFAULT_CONFIG from @morse-bot/morse-decoder for initial frequency/wpm values.

userStories.title: Add save session functionality to decoder
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to save a decoding session from the decoder panel.
acceptanceCriteria: In decoder-panel.tsx: add 'Save Session' Pressable button visible when authenticated and decodedText is non-empty
Button calls trpc.session.save mutation with: decodedText, durationMs (Date.now() - sessionStart), source ('mic' or 'file'), settings ({ targetFrequency, wpm })
Track session start time when recording/processing begins
Track last source ('mic' or 'file') based on which input method was used
Button shows 'Saving...' text and is disabled while mutation is pending
Show 'Sign in to save sessions' Pressable when not authenticated, triggers authClient.signIn.social({ provider: 'discord', callbackURL: '/' })
Show success Alert on save, error Alert on failure
Uses trpc from ~/utils/api and authClient from ~/utils/auth
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The tRPC session.save mutation is already available via the configured client. Follow the same mutation pattern used in the existing Expo app (see apps/expo/src/app/index.tsx for useMutation examples with trpc).

userStories.title: Create sessions list screen
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view, expand, export, and delete my saved sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/expo/src/app/sessions.tsx (replacing placeholder from US-004)
Auth guard: if authClient.useSession() has no session, show sign-in prompt with Discord OAuth button
Fetch sessions via useQuery(trpc.session.list.queryOptions())
Display sessions using LegendList (already a dependency) with: creation timestamp (toLocaleString), duration (formatted with formatDuration helper), source (mic/file, capitalized)
Each session shows first 100 chars of decodedText as preview with '...' if truncated
'Show more' / 'Show less' Pressable toggle for long sessions using expandedId state
Export button: write decodedText to file via expo-file-system then share via expo-sharing
Delete button: confirmation via Alert.alert('Delete this session?'), then trpc.session.delete mutation
Alert feedback on delete success/failure
Invalidate session queries after delete (queryClient.invalidateQueries)
Empty state: Text 'No saved sessions yet. Decode some morse code and click Save Session!'
formatDuration helper: formats ms to '1h 30m', '5m 30s', or '45s' (port from apps/nextjs/src/app/sessions/_components/sessions-list.tsx)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port logic from apps/nextjs/src/app/sessions/_components/sessions-list.tsx. Replace window.confirm with Alert.alert. Replace Blob download with expo-file-system writeAsStringAsync + expo-sharing shareAsync. Use LegendList (already imported in existing index.tsx) for the sessions list.

userStories.title: Polish theming and final cleanup
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to look polished with proper theming and no leftover demo code.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark/light mode works via useColorScheme() + NativeWind theme tokens throughout all screens
All components use consistent NativeWind classes matching the web app's color scheme (bg-background, text-foreground, bg-primary, etc.)
No remaining posts-related code anywhere in apps/expo/src/
No TypeScript errors: pnpm --filter @morse-bot/expo typecheck
No lint errors: pnpm --filter @morse-bot/expo lint
userStories.notes: The Tailwind theme tokens are already shared via @morse-bot/tailwind-config and imported in apps/expo/src/styles.css. NativeWind translates these to React Native styles. Verify all new components use the correct theme tokens.
